title: pedicularis kansuensis subsp. yargongensis
intro: Pedicularis kansuensis subsp. yargongensis is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae, commonly known as a type of broomrape. This plant has been traditionally used in Tibetan medicine, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which contribute to various health benefits such as reduced fever, relief from respiratory issues, and improved digestion. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are believed to provide relief from symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections, as well as to boost the immune system and promote overall well-being. In traditional medicine, Pedicularis kansuensis subsp. yargongensis is also used to treat conditions such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs, due to its expectorant properties that help to clear mucus from the lungs.
